title: Attendance and Participation
text: Students are expected to attend at least 75% of scheduled class meetings. Missing more than two consecutive sessions requires notifying the instructor and academic advisor. Approved absences include documented illness, religious observance, or university representation; evidence must be submitted within five business days.

title: Academic Integrity
text: Plagiarism, unauthorized collaboration, and falsification of data are violations of academic integrity. Faculty may use similarity checks and request drafts or notes. Sanctions range from a zero on the assessment to course failure and referral to the academic integrity board.

title: Late Submission
text: Assignments submitted after the deadline incur a 10% deduction per calendar day, up to five days. Work submitted more than five days late receives a zero unless an approved extension is on file. Technical issues are not grounds for late submission unless reported before the deadline.

title: Extensions
text: Extension requests must be submitted at least 48 hours before the due date and include supporting documentation. Extensions are granted for serious and temporary circumstances and may not exceed seven calendar days. Instructors document approved extensions in writing.

title: Examination Conduct
text: Students must present a valid student ID and follow proctor instructions during examinations. Electronic devices, notes, and unauthorized materials are prohibited unless explicitly permitted. Arriving more than 20 minutes late may result in denial of entry.

title: Examination Resit
text: A resit examination is available only when a final exam grade is between 40% and 49% and the course grade is at least 45%. The resit replaces only the final exam component and the maximum course grade after a resit is capped at 50%. Resits are scheduled within four weeks of grade release.

title: Grade Appeals
text: Grade appeals must be filed within 10 business days of grade release and must cite a procedural error or miscalculation. Appeals based solely on disagreement with academic judgment are not permitted. Decisions of the department chair are final unless new evidence is provided.

title: Academic Probation
text: Undergraduate students with a cumulative GPA below 2.0 for two consecutive terms are placed on academic probation. While on probation, students must meet with an advisor and enroll in a reduced course load. Failure to improve GPA in the next term may result in suspension.

title: Course Withdrawal
text: Students may withdraw from a course without academic penalty before 60% of the instructional period has elapsed. Withdrawals after this point require dean approval and appear as a W on the transcript. Tuition refunds follow the published refund schedule.

title: Incomplete Grade
text: An incomplete grade may be assigned only when a student has completed at least 70% of coursework and experiences a serious, documented disruption. The instructor and student must sign a completion plan with a due date not exceeding one term. Unresolved incompletes convert to an F.

title: Research Ethics
text: Research involving human participants, animals, or sensitive data requires prior ethics approval. Researchers must complete ethics training and maintain consent records for at least three years. Noncompliance may result in suspension of the project.

title: Disability Accommodations
text: Students seeking accommodations must register with the accessibility office and provide current documentation. Approved accommodations are communicated to instructors through official letters and must be implemented without altering essential course outcomes. Retroactive accommodations are not provided.

title: Course Load and Overload
text: A full-time course load is 12 to 18 credits per term. Requests to overload above 18 credits require a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or higher and advisor approval. Overload approval may be revoked if academic performance declines.

title: Graduation Requirements
text: To graduate, students must complete the required credits for their program, earn a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.0, and satisfy all general education requirements. A capstone project or thesis is required where specified by the program. All financial holds must be cleared before conferral.

title: Use of AI Tools
text: Use of AI tools for assignments is permitted only when explicitly allowed by the instructor. Students must disclose any AI assistance in a statement attached to the submission. Undisclosed or prohibited use may be treated as academic misconduct.

title: Data Privacy
text: University records, including grades and advising notes, are confidential and may be shared only with authorized personnel. Students should not email sensitive personal data unless using an approved secure channel. Data retention follows the institutional records schedule.

title: Library and Resource Use
text: Library resources are for academic use and require valid university credentials. Materials may be recalled if needed for course reserves, and overdue items incur daily fines. Persistent abuse of resources may result in temporary access suspension.

title: Harassment and Misconduct
text: Harassment, discrimination, and retaliation are prohibited in all academic settings. Reports are handled by the equity office and may trigger interim measures to protect participants. False reports made in bad faith are subject to disciplinary action.

title: Scholarship Maintenance
text: To maintain a merit scholarship, students must keep a minimum CGPA of 3.5. If the CGPA falls below this, the student is given one 'grace semester' to improve before the scholarship is revoked.

title: Course Repeat Policy
text: Students can repeat a course if they received a grade of D or F. The new grade will replace the old one in CGPA calculation, but the old grade will still appear on the transcript.

title: Credit Hour Transfer
text: Credits from other accredited universities may be transferred if the grade is C or higher. A maximum of 50% of program credits can be transferred.

title: Semester Freeze
text: A student can freeze a semester for medical or personal reasons for up to one year. A formal application must be submitted to the Registrar before the mid-term exams.

title: Official Transcripts
text: Official transcripts can be requested via the student portal. A processing fee applies, and it takes 5-7 business days to issue the document.

title: Tuition Fee Deadlines
text: Tuition fees must be paid by the 10th of the first month of each semester. A late fee of $50 is charged for payments made after the deadline.

title: Hostel Night curfew
text: Hostel residents must return to the premises by 10:00 PM. Late entry requires prior written permission from the Warden and a valid reason.

title: Laboratory Access
text: Students can only use science or computer labs during scheduled hours or with a supervisor's permission. Lab safety gear (coats/goggles) is mandatory.

title: Student Council Elections
text: Elections for the Student Council are held every October. Only students with a CGPA above 2.5 and no disciplinary record are eligible to contest.

title: Library Borrowing Limits
text: Undergraduate students can borrow up to 5 books for 14 days. Overdue books incur a fine of $1 per day.

title: Medical Leave Policy
text: Medical leave for more than 3 days requires a certificate from the University Medical Officer or a registered hospital, submitted within 48 hours of return.

title: Change of Major
text: Students can apply to change their major after completing the first year, provided they meet the minimum GPA requirements of the new department.

title: Internship Credit Requirements
text: Internships must be at least 6 weeks long and approved by the department head to earn academic credit. A final report and employer feedback are mandatory.

title: Campus Parking Permits
text: All student vehicles must display a valid parking permit. Unauthorized parking in faculty areas will result in a fine of $30.

title: Disciplinary Action
text: Violations of the student code of conduct, including fighting or vandalism, may lead to suspension or permanent expulsion after a hearing.

title: Add/Drop Period
text: Students can add or drop courses during the first two weeks of the semester without any financial or academic penalty.

title: Campus Wi-Fi Access
text: University Wi-Fi is for academic use. Users must login with their student ID. Accessing blocked or illegal websites will lead to account suspension.

title: Lost and Found
text: Items found on campus should be deposited at the Security Office. Unclaimed items are auctioned or donated after 90 days.

title: Gym and Fitness Center
text: The university gym is free for all registered students. Students must bring their own towels and wear proper athletic shoes.

title: Degree Conferral
text: Degrees are conferred once a year during the Annual Convocation. All dues must be cleared 30 days before the event to be eligible.

title: Email Policy
text: The official university email is the primary mode of communication. Students are responsible for all notices sent to their university inbox.

title: Student Employment
text: On-campus jobs are available for students with a GPA above 3.0. Maximum working hours are 20 hours per week during the semester.

title: Guest Policy
text: Guests are not allowed in classrooms or labs without prior permission from the instructor. In hostels, guests must leave by 8:00 PM.

title: Plagiarism Check
text: All major assignments must be submitted through 'Turnitin'. A similarity score of over 20% may result in rejection of the work.

title: Academic Advising
text: Every student is assigned an Academic Advisor. Students must meet their advisor at least once a semester for course approval.

title: Fee Refund Policy
text: 100% refund is available if withdrawal is in the first week, 50% in the second week, and no refund after the third week of the semester.

title: Anti-Ragging Policy
text: Ragging (bullying/hazing) of new students is strictly prohibited. Involved students face immediate expulsion and legal action.

title: Emergency Contacts
text: In case of fire or medical emergency, students should call the Campus Security Helpline at extension 999.

title: Course Evaluation
text: Students must complete online course evaluations at the end of each term before they can access their final grades.

title: Student Clubs Membership
text: Students can join up to three active clubs. Membership is open at the start of each semester during 'Club Fair'.

title: Anti-Ragging Policy

title: Emergency Contacts
text: In case of fire or medical emergency, students should call the Campus Security Helpline at extension 999.

title: Course Evaluation
text: Students must complete online course evaluations at the end of each term before they can access their final grades.

title: Student Clubs Membership
text: Students can join up to three active clubs. Membership is open at the start of each semester during 'Club Fair'.

title: Tuition Installment Plan
text: Standard tuition fees are paid in 2 installments. However, if you want 3 installments, you must provide needy papers and a proven reason or documentation for financial hardship to the finance office.

title: Semester Freeze Policy
text: A student can freeze their semester due to medical reasons or personal emergency for a maximum of two semesters. You must submit a formal application before the mid-term exams. To unfreeze, a re-joining form must be submitted 15 days before the new semester starts.

title: Add and Drop Subjects
text: Students can add or drop subjects during the first two weeks of the semester (Add/Drop week). Dropping a subject after this period will result in a 'W' (Withdrawal) grade on the transcript and no fee refund will be provided.

title: Pre-requisite Subjects
text: You cannot add an advanced subject unless you have passed its pre-requisite. If you fail a pre-requisite, any advanced subject added will be automatically dropped by the system.

title: Degree Verification Process
text: To verify a degree, students must submit a copy of their final transcript and CNIC to the Registrar's Office. Internal verification takes 10 business days. For external verification (HEC), students must apply through the official HEC portal and then get it attested by the university.

title: Convocation Eligibility
text: Students are eligible for convocation only if they have cleared all academic requirements, have a minimum CGPA of 2.0, and have zero outstanding financial dues. Registration for convocation must be done at least 45 days before the event.

title: Convocation Gown and Photography
text: Graduating students must wear the official university gown and hood during the ceremony. Gowns can be rented for a deposit fee of $50, which is refundable upon return. Private photographers are not allowed on the main stage.

title: Degree Issuance (Urgent)
text: Standard degree issuance takes 30 days after convocation. However, students can apply for an 'Urgent Degree' by paying an extra fee of $100, and it will be processed within 7 business days.

title: Medals and Academic Awards
text: Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals are awarded during convocation to students with the highest CGPAs in their respective departments. To be eligible, a student must not have failed any subject or repeated any course during their entire degree.

title: Computer Science (CS) Course Offerings
text: The BS Computer Science program offers core subjects including Programming Fundamentals, Data Structures, Algorithms, Operating Systems, and Database Systems. Electives include Web Development and Mobile App Development.

title: Software Engineering (SE) Course Offerings
text: The BS Software Engineering program focuses on Software Requirement Engineering, Software Design and Architecture, Software Quality Assurance, and Project Management. Students must complete a Capstone Project in their final year.

title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Course Offerings
text: The BS AI program offers specialized courses in Machine Learning, Neural Networks, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Computer Vision, and Robotics. Strong foundations in Calculus and Linear Algebra are required.

title: Credit Hours per Subject
text: Most core technical subjects are 4 credit hours (3 hours theory + 1 hour lab). General education subjects like Pakistan Studies or Islamic Studies are 2 to 3 credit hours.

title: Prerequisite Rule for Subjects
text: Students cannot enroll in 'Programming II' without passing 'Programming I'. Similarly, 'Machine Learning' requires passing 'Python for AI' and 'Statistics' first.

title: Capstone Project Requirement
text: All final year students in CS, SE, and AI programs must complete a Capstone Project under faculty supervision. The project should demonstrate practical application of learned concepts and is worth 6 credit hours.

title: Internship Requirement
text: Students must complete a minimum of 8 weeks of internship in a relevant industry during their degree program. The internship must be approved by the department and is worth 3 credit hours.

title: Elective Subject Selection
text: Students can choose elective subjects from a predefined list provided by the department. Electives must align with the student's major and require advisor approval.

title: Subject Withdrawal Policy
text: Students may withdraw from a subject before the mid-term exams without academic penalty. After mid-terms, withdrawal will result in a 'W' grade on the transcript.

title: Lab Attendance Requirement
text: Attendance in lab sessions is mandatory for all technical subjects. Students must attend at least 80% of lab sessions to qualify for final assessment in that subject.

title: General Scholarship Overview
text: Riphah International University provides various scholarships, with approximately 20% of its student body receiving some form of financial aid. The university offers numerous schemes rather than a single fixed number. Key opportunities include Talent, Merit, Kinship, and Hifz-ul-Quran scholarships.

title: Merit-Based Scholarships
text: Talent Scholarship offers 100% (80%+ marks), 50% (75-79.99%), or 25% (70-74.99%) off tuition fees for new Bachelor/MS students. Salam Scholarship provides up to Rs. 20,000 for top 1st-5th merit holders in BS programs. Mumtaz Scholarship is also available for high-achieving current students.

title: Need-Based and Assistantships
text: Wali Assistantship provides a 50% tuition fee reduction for orphaned students (requires SGPA 3.20+). Need-Based Scholarships are decided by a committee. Zulfiqar and Siddique Assistantships are for faculty/staff dependents (40% to 100% reduction). Mawakhat Assistantship is for current students based on need-cum-merit.

title: Family and Specialized Scholarships
text: Kinship (Usra) Scholarship offers 10% for the 2nd sibling and 20% for the 3rd enrolled concurrently. Hifz-ul-Quran Scholarship offers 10% for certified students. Special schemes include 10% off for Alumni children, Dar-e-Arqam, and Ummah International School students. Special 100% scholarships exist for flood-affected regions.

title: Scholarship General Requirements
text: To apply for any scholarship, students must have no outstanding dues. Applications must be signed by a parent/guardian with proof of need or performance. Scholarships are generally not applicable for repeated courses or the first semester (unless specified for new admissions).

title: Scholarship Maintenance Criteria
text: Merit scholarships require maintaining a CGPA of 3.0 or higher. Falling below this results in a 'grace semester' to improve. Need-based scholarships may require periodic re-evaluation of financial status. All scholarship recipients must adhere to university conduct policies.

title: Student Parking Fines
text: Parking in a faculty-reserved spot results in a $30 fine. Repeat offenders (more than 3 times) will have their campus parking permit revoked for the entire semester.

title: Alumni Library Access
text: Graduated students can access the university library by applying for an 'Alumni Library Card' for a yearly fee of $50. Borrowing privileges are limited to 2 books.

title: Lost and Found Procedure
text: Found items must be turned into the security office. If an item is not claimed within 90 days, it is either auctioned for charity or disposed of.

title: Credit Hour Transfer Grade
text: When transferring credits from another university, only courses with a grade of 'C' or higher are accepted. The grades do not impact the Riphah CGPA.

title: Official Social Media Conduct
text: Students are prohibited from using the university name or logo to create unofficial social media pages. Doing so without permission leads to disciplinary action.

title: Group Project Conflict Resolution
text: In case of disputes in group projects, students must first contact their instructor. If unresolved, the case is referred to the Departmental Student Coordinator.

title: Summer Internship Credit
text: Summer internships must be at least 8 weeks long to qualify for 3 credit hours. A completion certificate and a supervisor's report are required.

title: Double Major Policy
text: Students with a CGPA above 3.7 can apply for a double major after their second year, subject to approval from both department heads.

title: Cafeteria Food Safety Complaints
text: Any hygiene issues in the cafeteria should be reported to the Campus Health Officer. Samples are sent for testing if a formal complaint is filed by 5+ students.

title: Printing and Photocopy Services
text: The library provides printing services at a subsidized rate. Students get 50 free pages per semester; additional pages are charged at $0.05 per page.

title: Student Employment Hours
text: Students working in on-campus part-time jobs (e.g., Lab Assistant) are limited to 20 hours per week during the semester.

title: Academic Warning
text: An academic warning is issued to students whose term GPA falls below 2.0. This is a precursor to academic probation if performance doesn't improve.

title: Lab Equipment Insurance
text: High-value equipment (like Robotics kits) is insured by the university. However, students must sign a liability form before checking out equipment for home use.

title: Course Evaluation Participation
text: Course evaluations are mandatory. Students who do not complete the online evaluation by the deadline will have their online result portal locked.

title: Campus Prayer Room Conduct
text: Prayer rooms are for worship only. Sleeping, eating, or holding group discussions in the prayer area is strictly prohibited.

title: Emergency Evacuation Drill
text: Fire and evacuation drills are conducted once a semester. Participation is mandatory for all students, faculty, and staff present on campus.

title: Research Assistant Eligibility
text: Students with a 3.5+ CGPA and strong interest in research can apply to be Research Assistants. They receive a monthly stipend and a certificate.

title: English Language Requirement
text: Students from non-English speaking backgrounds must pass the University English Proficiency Test (UEPT) or provide a valid IELTS score of 6.0.

title: Guest House Booking
text: Parents of out-station students can book the university guest house for a maximum of 3 days. Booking must be done through the Warden's office 1 week in advance.

title: Laptop Policy in Lectures
text: Laptops are allowed for note-taking purposes only. Using laptops for gaming or movies during class results in immediate confiscation for the day.

title: Final Year Thesis Submission
text: Thesis must be submitted in both hard-bound (3 copies) and digital (PDF) formats. Plagiarism check via Turnitin is mandatory before submission.

title: Blood Donation Drives
text: The university organizes two blood donation camps annually. Student volunteers receive 'Community Service' certificates for their participation.

title: Semester Break Duration
text: The break between Fall and Spring semesters is typically 2 weeks, while the summer break is 8 to 10 weeks long.

title: Late Registration Fee
text: Students who register for courses after the Add/Drop week must pay a late registration penalty of $100.

title: International Student Visa Support
text: The International Student Office provides NOCs and documentation support for student visa renewals. Requests take 5 business days to process.

title: Gym Membership for Faculty
text: The university gym is available for faculty members from 6:00 AM to 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM. Entry is free with a staff ID.

title: Departmental Change Fee
text: Switching from one department to another (e.g., CS to AI) involves a processing fee of $75 and is subject to merit seat availability.

title: Use of Personal Electrical Appliances
text: In hostels, high-wattage appliances like heaters or electric kettles are prohibited due to fire safety. Using them results in a $50 fine.

title: Public Speaking Club (Declamation)
text: The declamation society holds monthly competitions. Winners represent the university in Inter-University debating contests.

title: NOC for External Internships
text: Students requiring a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for external internships must apply through the Career Services portal.

title: Fee Concession for Orphans
text: In addition to Wali Assistantship, orphans are eligible for a 100% discount on library and laboratory fees.

title: Campus Photography Permit
text: Professional photography or videography for commercial purposes on campus requires a permit from the PR and Communications department.

title: Plagiarism Score for Assignments
text: Regular class assignments must have a similarity index below 15% on Turnitin to be accepted for grading.

title: Re-checking of Exam Papers
text: Students can apply for paper re-checking by paying $20 per subject. Re-checking only covers counting errors or unmarked questions.

title: Student Mentor Program
text: Senior students with 3.2+ CGPA can become 'Peer Mentors' for freshmen. Mentors are trained to help new students settle into university life.

title: Medical Insurance for Students
text: All full-time students are covered under the university's basic health insurance policy for on-campus accidents.

title: Course Withdrawal (W Grade)
text: A 'W' grade does not affect the CGPA but remains on the transcript. It indicates the student opted out after the Add/Drop deadline.

title: Degree Attestation Support
text: The university provides attestation for HEC purposes. Students must bring original degrees and transcripts to the Registrar’s office on Tuesdays or Thursdays.

title: Campus Wi-Fi Data Limit
text: Students have a daily data limit of 2GB on the university Wi-Fi. Excessive usage for non-academic purposes may lead to temporary bandwidth throttling.

title: Use of Lab Software
text: Licensed software (Matlab, AutoCAD, etc.) is only for academic use. Copying licensed software for personal use is a violation of the IT policy.

title: Convocation Rehearsal
text: Attendance at the convocation rehearsal (usually one day before) is mandatory. Students who skip the rehearsal may not be allowed to participate in the main event.

title: Hostel Room Allotment
text: Rooms are allotted on a merit-first basis (priority to far-away residents and high CGPA students). Room swapping is not allowed without Warden approval.

title: Anti-Harassment Committee
text: The university has a dedicated committee for sexual harassment complaints. All reports are strictly confidential and investigated within 30 days.

title: Credit Hour Cap for Graduating Students
text: Students in their final semester can request to take up to 21 credit hours (with dean approval) if it allows them to complete their degree.

title: University Bus Route Suggestion
text: Students can suggest new bus routes by collecting at least 25 signatures from students living in that specific area.

title: Disability Access (Ramps/Elevators)
text: All new campus buildings are equipped with ramps and elevators. Students with physical disabilities have priority access to these facilities.

title: Fee Waiver for Sports Excellence
text: National-level athletes can apply for a sports scholarship, which provides a 50% to 100% tuition waiver based on their performance level.

title: Annual Research Journal Submission
text: The university publishes an undergraduate research journal. Best student papers are awarded a cash prize of $200.

title: Psychological Wellness Workshops
text: The Student Affairs office conducts monthly workshops on stress management, exam anxiety, and time management.

title: Degree Conferral in Absentia
text: Students who cannot attend the convocation can receive their degree 'in absentia' by applying after the ceremony and paying a postal fee.

title: Internship Duration Extension
text: If a student wishes to extend their internship beyond 8 weeks, they must get written approval from their faculty advisor to ensure it doesn't clash with the next semester.

title: Elective Subject Swapping
text: Students can swap an elective subject within the first 10 days of the semester, provided the new elective has available seats and no timetable clash.

title: Student Publication Rights
text: Research conducted using university lab resources is the joint intellectual property of the student and the university. Formal permission is needed before external publication.

title: Incomplete Grade Deadline
text: An 'I' (Incomplete) grade must be resolved within the first 4 weeks of the following semester. Otherwise, the portal automatically converts it into an 'F' grade.

title: Campus Event Security
text: All student-organized events must have at least two faculty chaperones and must conclude by 6:00 PM unless special late-night permission is granted.

title: Duplicate Transcript Fee
text: If the original transcript is lost, a duplicate can be issued for a fee of $25. The word 'Duplicate' will be clearly watermarked on the new document.

title: Industrial Visit Attendance
text: Attendance during mandatory industrial visits is marked as 'Present' for all missed classes on that day. Students must submit a visit report within 48 hours.

title: Student Society Elections
text: Elections for society presidents are held annually. Candidates must have a minimum CGPA of 3.0 and no history of disciplinary warnings.

title: Lost Library Book Penalty
text: If a student loses a library book, they must either replace it with a new copy of the same edition or pay three times the current market price of the book.

title: Official Email Etiquette
text: Students must use their official university email for all formal correspondence. Faculty are not required to respond to queries sent from personal Gmail or Yahoo accounts.

title: Final Year Project (FYP) Mentor Change
text: A change of FYP supervisor is only allowed once, and it must be requested within the first 4 weeks of the project start date.

title: Summer Semester Fee Policy
text: Summer courses are charged on a per-credit-hour basis. No scholarships or discounts (except Kinship) are applicable during the summer term.

title: Class Representative (CR) Duties
text: CRs are responsible for coordinating between the class and the instructor. They can be removed from the position if their own attendance falls below 80%.

title: Hostel Laundry Services
text: Hostel residents are provided laundry services twice a week. A maximum of 10 items per week is allowed; exceeding this results in an extra charge.

title: Credit Hour Transfer Limit
text: A maximum of 60 credit hours can be transferred from an HEC-recognized university towards a 4-year degree program at Riphah.

title: Medical Fitness Certificate
text: New admissions in specialized programs like Robotics or Medical Lab Sciences must submit a medical fitness certificate from a registered practitioner.

title: Use of University Auditorium
text: The main auditorium must be booked at least 15 days in advance through the Student Affairs office. A security deposit is required for non-academic events.

title: Course Syllabus Access
text: Complete course outlines, including grading rubrics and reading lists, must be uploaded to the LMS (Learning Management System) by the instructor before the first class.

title: Student Discount on Software
text: Students can get free access to Microsoft Office 365 and Adobe Creative Cloud using their university email ID through the IT portal.

title: Campus Canteen Operating Hours
text: The main campus canteen operates from 8:30 AM to 8:30 PM. Food service is restricted during official university prayer timings.

title: Semester GPA Calculation
text: Semester GPA is calculated using the quality points of each grade. In case of a repeat course, only the higher grade is used in the final CGPA calculation.

title: Lab Report Submission Policy
text: Hard copies of lab reports must be submitted at the start of the next lab session. Late submissions are penalized 20% per day.

title: University Identity (Logo) Usage
text: Unauthorized printing of the university logo on t-shirts or merchandise for sale is a legal offense and leads to a $200 fine.

title: Final Year Thesis Defense
text: Public defense of the thesis is required for all MS and PhD students. BS students have a closed-room viva with an internal and external examiner.

title: Emergency Leave for Staff Students
text: Students who are also university employees are allowed 3 days of paid emergency leave per semester for family crises.

title: Online Class Protocol
text: In case of online sessions, students must keep their cameras on during quizzes. Failure to do so results in the quiz being marked as void.

title: Course Challenge Exam
text: Students with prior professional experience can 'challenge' a course by taking a comprehensive exam. If they pass with 80%+, they earn the credits without attending classes.

title: Alumni Convocation Guest Fee
text: Alumni attending the convocation after their graduating year must pay a guest fee of $30 per person.

title: Parking for Heavy Vehicles
text: Commercial or heavy vehicles (like delivery trucks) are only allowed on campus between 10:00 PM and 6:00 AM.

title: Student Health Record Privacy
text: Medical records submitted for leave are strictly confidential and are only accessible by the University Medical Officer and the Dean.

title: Mandatory 75 Percent Attendance
text: A minimum of 75% attendance is strictly required in each subject. This is compulsory to be eligible to sit for the final examination. Without 75% attendance, you will not be allowed to take the paper under any circumstances.

title: Examination Barring Policy
text: Students whose attendance falls below 75% in a course will be automatically barred by the system from appearing in the final exam of that specific subject. No admit card will be issued for such courses.

title: Shortage of Attendance
text: Shortage of attendance (below 75%) cannot be compensated by extra assignments or projects. The only way to sit for the exam is to meet the 75% physical presence requirement in classes.

title: Attendance Calculation for Papers
text: Attendance for paper eligibility is calculated from the first day of classes until the last teaching week. Students must track their portal to ensure they are above the 75% threshold before the date sheet is released.

title: Late Admission Attendance
text: Students who take admission late are still required to meet the 75% attendance criteria. Missed classes prior to admission will be counted as absent.

title: Medical Leave and Exam Eligibility
text: Even with an approved medical leave, a student's absolute attendance must not fall below 60% to be considered for a special waiver to take the paper. Standard requirement remains 75%.

title: Attendance in Lab Sessions
text: For subjects with a lab component, 75% attendance in both theory and lab sessions is mandatory. Failure in either attendance count will result in being barred from the final practical exam.

title: Warning for Low Attendance
text: The Registrar's office issues a warning letter when a student's attendance drops below 80%, reminding them that falling below 75% will result in exam disqualification.

title: Attendance Re-counting Appeal
text: If a student believes their attendance was marked incorrectly, they must appeal to the HOD within 3 days of the record being posted. After this, the 75% record is finalized for exam seating.

title: Duty Leave Impact on 75 Percent Rule
text: Duty leave for university events is the only way to maintain the 75% count if a student is away. Proper documentation must be signed by the Dean to ensure eligibility for papers.

title: Attendance Monitoring Responsibility
text: It is the student's responsibility to regularly check their attendance status via the student portal. Ignorance of low attendance will not be accepted as a valid excuse for being barred from exams.

title: Final Exam Eligibility Confirmation
text: Before the final exam schedule is released, students must confirm their eligibility based on attendance. The university will not entertain last-minute requests for exam participation due to attendance issues.

title: Impact of Online Classes on Attendance
text: In cases where classes are held online, attendance is still tracked and counted towards the 75% requirement. Students must log in and participate actively to ensure their presence is recorded.

title: Computing Paper Conduct and PBL
text: For computing students, before the final theory papers, PBL (Project Based Learning) is conducted. In PBL, students must represent their projects in front of a panel. This panel evaluation is mandatory to qualify for the final theory examination.

title: PBL Panel Presentation
text: PBL presentations require students to demonstrate their software or hardware projects to a specialized panel. You must defend your project's logic and implementation before you are allowed to sit for the final paper.

title: Eligibility for Final Theory Paper
text: To attend the final theory paper, a student must pass the PBL phase. Failure to represent or pass the project in front of the panel will lead to disqualification from the final examination.

title: PBL Grading Impact
text: The grade obtained in the PBL presentation is separate from the final theory paper grade but is a prerequisite for sitting in the final exam. A fail in PBL means you cannot take the final paper, regardless of your theory marks.

title: PBL Resubmission Policy
text: If a student fails the PBL presentation, they may be allowed to resubmit or re-present their project within a specified timeframe. However, this is subject to approval by the department and does not guarantee eligibility for the final paper.

title: PBL Documentation Submission
text: Students must submit all required documentation and project reports before their PBL presentation. Incomplete submissions will lead to disqualification from both the PBL evaluation and the final theory exam.

title: PBL Panel Composition
text: The PBL evaluation panel typically consists of faculty members specialized in the relevant computing field. Their decision regarding a student's eligibility for the final paper is final and binding.

title: PBL Feedback and Improvement
text: After the PBL presentation, students receive feedback from the panel. While this feedback is valuable for future projects, it does not affect eligibility for the current final theory examination.

title: PBL and Final Exam Scheduling
text: PBL presentations are scheduled prior to the final theory exams. Students must ensure they are available for their assigned PBL slots to avoid conflicts with exam dates.

title: Examination Admit Card Requirement
text: Having an Admit Card is mandatory to sit for the examination. You cannot take the paper without it. If you do not have an admit card, you will not be able to take the papers.

title: How to Generate Admit Card
text: To get your admit card, you must visit the university's official 'Fiori' portal. From there, you can generate and download your admit card for the examinations.

title: Portal Fiori Access for Exams
text: Students must log in to the Fiori portal using their official credentials to verify their exam eligibility and download the mandatory admit card before the exam session starts.
